claim: Set up your second brain with simple initial structure (30-40 documents) rather than trying to achieve perfection; it will grow naturally to hundreds of documents over weeks
actionable: Start your AI operating system with minimal context; don't aim for completeness on day one, let it compound organically through use
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: Claude.md files with system instructions and folder structure mapping are critical for efficient agent context retrieval and must be co-located with the vault
actionable: Create Claude.md files that tell agents exactly where to find and store information; structure them hierarchically for your vault to scale
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: Scheduled tasks pulling real-time context (meetings, Slack, analytics) automatically update your second brain daily with current business state
actionable: Set up daily scheduled tasks using connectors (Fireflies, Slack, Circle) to keep your vault current with live business context
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: Second brains grow complex and inefficient over time; running an audit/optimizer skill weekly or bi-weekly maintains health and token efficiency
actionable: Schedule weekly optimizer runs using frameworks like Caveman Compression, Chroma Context Rot, and Karpathy's N&M wiki to maintain vault quality
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: Obsidian Relay plugin with custom permission layers (owner vs member roles) enables real-time team sync of shared vaults with read/write controls
actionable: Deploy Ben AI Relay plugin on Obsidian with role-based access control so teams share context without accidentally modifying critical docs
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: MCPs (Model Context Protocols) add a complexity layer that degrades accuracy of context retrieval and costs more tokens than direct folder access
actionable: For performance-critical systems, store second brains in local folders with Claude.md files rather than cloud-based MCPs via Google Drive or Notion
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)

claim: Routines (cloud-based agents) allow scheduled OS operator and optimizer tasks to run autonomously even when laptop is closed, unlike scheduled tasks
actionable: Set up Claude routines with second brain MCP to run vault updates and optimizations 24/7 without manual intervention or laptop dependency
source_title: 5 Skills to Build an AI Operating System Like The 1% (Full Guide)
